rawIntent:
The CI pipeline failed with the following TypeScript errors:
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto.ts:13 — Unable to resolve signature of property decorator when called as an expression.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto.ts:10 — Unable to resolve signature of property decorator when called as an expression.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto.ts:7 — Unable to resolve signature of property decorator when called as an expression.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto.ts:4 — Unable to resolve signature of property decorator when called as an expression.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:77 — 'request.params' is of type 'unknown'.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:68 — 'request.params' is of type 'unknown'.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:59 — Argument of type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:59 — 'request.params' is of type 'unknown'.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:47 — 'request.params' is of type 'unknown'.
- src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts:35 — Argument of type 'import("/home/runner/work/trackeros/trackeros/src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto").CreateLeaveRequestDto' is not assignable to parameter of type 'import("/home/runner/work/trackeros/trackeros/src/modules/leave/leave.model").CreateLeaveRequestDto'.

The project's tsconfig.json does not have experimentalDecorators enabled, which causes the class-validator decorators in leave.dto.ts to fail compilation. Additionally, the leave controller uses FastifyRequest generic types that are not recognized by the installed Fastify version, leading to 'unknown' params and a type mismatch when passing the DTO to ILeaveManagementService.submitLeaveRequest (the service expects a CreateLeaveRequestDto from leave.model, not the class from leave.dto.ts).

Preserve all existing exports, types, interfaces, and imports. Only add or modify what is needed to resolve the CI failure shown above.

successCriteria.description: experimentalDecorators is enabled in tsconfig.json and all decorator-related compilation errors in leave.dto.ts are resolved.
Fastify request generic types in leave.controller.ts are corrected so that request.params is properly typed (no 'unknown' errors) and the argument type matches the expected service signature.
DTO imports in leave.controller.ts are consistent: the CreateLeaveRequestDto used in the controller matches the type expected by ILeaveManagementService.submitLeaveRequest (from leave.model).
The CI pipeline passes with zero TypeScript errors related to the listed files.

constraints: Preserve all existing exports, types, interfaces, and imports in the affected files.
Only modify files necessary to resolve the CI errors (leave.dto.ts, leave.controller.ts, tsconfig.json, and any route files if needed).
Correct Fastify request types to match the installed Fastify version and the expected service signatures.
Ensure DTO imports are consistent: the controller must use the same CreateLeaveRequestDto type that the service expects (from leave.model).
No changes to business logic, service contracts, or leave request lifecycle.
Adhere to existing architecture: dependency direction leave -> employee, leave -> policy, leave -> balance, leave -> notification; no reverse dependencies.
All modifications must not introduce new linting or type errors.
Repository pattern: all database access goes through repository interfaces (not directly relevant to this fix, but must not be violated).
Input validation: validate all inputs at API boundaries (existing validation logic must remain intact).
No sensitive data in logs: ensure no logging of passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data (no new logging introduced).
RBAC enforcement: all API endpoints enforce role-based access control (existing guards must remain).
Error handling: no unhandled promise rejections; all async errors are caught and handled (existing error handling must remain).
outOfScope: Modifying leave module business logic or service implementations.
Changing the leave request lifecycle (Pending, Approved, Rejected).
Adding new endpoints, features, or functionality.
Modifying employee, policy, balance, or notification modules.
Updating package dependencies (unless strictly required to resolve type errors, but not indicated).
Any files outside src/modules/leave-api/ and tsconfig.json.
